children.name: Hylonympha
children.description: Hylonympha — a genus of hummingbirds in the family Trochilidae.

children.name: Hylonympha macrocerca
children.description: The scissor-tailed hummingbird is an Endangered species of hummingbird in the "mountain gems", tribe Lampornithini in subfamily Trochilinae. It is endemic to Venezuela.

children.name: Eulidia
children.description: Eulidia — a genus of hummingbirds in the family Trochilidae.

children.name: Eulidia yarrellii
children.description: The Chilean woodstar is a Critically Endangered species of hummingbird in tribe Mellisugini of subfamily Trochilinae, the "bee hummingbirds". It is the only species placed in the genus Eulidia. It is endemic to Chile though there are unconfirmed reports from southern Peru.

children.name: Stephanoxis
children.description: Stephanoxis — a genus of hummingbirds in the family Trochilidae.

children.name: Stephanoxis lalandi
children.description: The green-crowned plovercrest, also black-breasted plovercrest or simply plovercrest, is a species of hummingbird in the "emeralds", tribe Trochilini of subfamily Trochilinae. It is endemic to Brazil.

children.name: Stephanoxis loddigesii
children.description: The purple-crowned plovercrest, or violet-crowned plovercrest, is a species of hummingbird in the "emeralds", tribe Trochilini of subfamily Trochilinae. It is found in Argentina, Brazil, and Paraguay.

children.name: Ramphomicron
children.description: Ramphomicron — a genus of hummingbirds in the family Trochilidae.

children.name: Ramphomicron dorsale
children.description: The black-backed thornbill is a Near Threatened species of hummingbird in the "coquettes", tribe Lesbiini of subfamily Lesbiinae. It is endemic to the Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta of northern Colombia.

children.name: Ramphomicron microrhynchum
children.description: The purple-backed thornbill is a species of hummingbird in the "coquettes", tribe Lesbiini of subfamily Lesbiinae. It is found in Bolivia, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, and Venezuela.

children.name: Chlorostilbon
children.description: Chlorostilbon — a genus of hummingbirds in the family Trochilidae.

children.name: Chlorostilbon gibsoni
children.description: The red-billed emerald is a species of hummingbird in the "emeralds", tribe Trochilini of subfamily Trochilinae. It is found in Colombia and Venezuela.

children.name: Chlorostilbon poortmani
children.description: The short-tailed emerald is a species of hummingbird in the "emeralds", tribe Trochilini of subfamily Trochilinae. It is found in Colombia and Venezuela. It has also been called Poortman's emerald hummingbird.

children.name: Chlorostilbon russatus
children.description: The coppery emerald is a species of hummingbird in the "emeralds", tribe Trochilini of subfamily Trochilinae. It is found in Colombia and Venezuela.

children.name: Chlorostilbon stenurus
children.description: The narrow-tailed emerald is a species of hummingbird in the "emeralds", tribe Trochilini of subfamily Trochilinae. It is found in Colombia and Venezuela.

children.name: Thalurania
children.description: Thalurania — a genus of hummingbirds in the family Trochilidae.

children.name: Thalurania colombica
children.description: The crowned woodnymph or violet-crowned woodnymph, is a species of hummingbird in the "emeralds", tribe Trochilini of subfamily Trochilinae. It is found from Belize and Guatemala to far-northern Peru.

children.name: Thalurania furcata
children.description: The fork-tailed woodnymph is a species of hummingbird in the "emeralds", tribe Trochilini of subfamily Trochilinae. It is found in every mainland South American country except Chile and Uruguay.

children.name: Thalurania glaucopis
children.description: The violet-capped woodnymph is a species of hummingbird in the "emeralds", tribe Trochilini of subfamily Trochilinae. It is found in Argentina, Brazil, Paraguay, and Uruguay.

children.name: Calothorax
children.description: Calothorax — a genus of hummingbirds in the family Trochilidae.

children.name: Calothorax lucifer
children.description: The lucifer sheartail or lucifer hummingbird is a medium-sized, 10 cm long, green hummingbird with a slightly curved bill and distinctive outward flare of its gorget feathers. Its habitat is in high-altitude areas of northern Mexico and southwestern United States. It winters in central Mexico.

children.name: Calothorax pulcher
children.description: The beautiful sheartail or beautiful hummingbird is a species of hummingbird in tribe Mellisugini of subfamily Trochilinae, the "bee hummingbirds". It is endemic to Mexico.

children.name: Phaethornis
children.description: Phaethornis — a genus of hummingbirds in the family Trochilidae.

children.name: Phaethornis bourcieri
children.description: The straight-billed hermit is a species of bird in the family Trochilidae, the hummingbirds. It is found in Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, French Guiana, Guyana, Peru, Suriname, and Venezuela.

children.name: Phaethornis longuemareus
children.description: The little hermit is a hummingbird that is a resident breeder in north-eastern Venezuela, northern Guyana, Suriname, French Guiana and Trinidad. This lowland species occurs in various semi-open wooded habitats, e.g. mangrove, secondary forest, plantations and scrub.

children.name: Phaethornis major
children.description: Phaethornis major — a species in the genus Phaethornis, family Trochilidae. Also known as hummingbirds.

children.name: Phaethornis nattereri
children.description: The cinnamon-throated hermit is a species in the hummingbird family Trochilidae. It is found in Bolivia and Brazil.

children.name: Phaethornis rupurumii
children.description: The streak-throated hermit, also known as the Rupurumi hermit, is a species of hummingbird in the family Trochilidae. It is found in Brazil, Colombia, Guyana, and Venezuela.

children.name: Phaethornis squalidus
children.description: The dusky-throated hermit is a species of hummingbird in the family Trochilidae. It is endemic to Brazil.

children.name: Phaethornis superciliosus
children.description: The long-tailed hermit is a large hummingbird that is a resident breeder in Venezuela, the Guianas, and north-eastern Brazil. This species was formerly referred to as the eastern long-tailed hermit.

children.name: Heliodoxa jacula
children.description: The green-crowned brilliant is species of hummingbird in the "brilliants", tribe Heliantheini in subfamily Lesbiinae. It is found in Colombia, Costa Rica, Ecuador, and Panama. It is also known as the green-fronted brilliant.

children.name: Coeligena albicaudata
children.description: Coeligena albicaudata — a species in the genus Coeligena, family Trochilidae. Also known as hummingbirds.

children.name: Coeligena coeligena
children.description: The bronzy inca is a species of hummingbird in the "brilliants", tribe Heliantheini in subfamily Lesbiinae. It is found in Bolivia, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, and Venezuela.

children.name: Coeligena consita
children.description: The Perija starfrontlet is an Endangered species of hummingbird in the "brilliants", tribe Heliantheini in subfamily Lesbiinae. It is found in Colombia and Venezuela.

children.name: Coeligena eisenmanni
children.description: Coeligena eisenmanni — a species in the genus Coeligena, family Trochilidae. Also known as hummingbirds.

children.name: Coeligena helianthea
children.description: The blue-throated starfrontlet is a species of hummingbird in the "brilliants", tribe Heliantheini in subfamily Lesbiinae. It is found in Colombia and Venezuela.

children.name: Coeligena iris
children.description: The rainbow starfrontlet is a species of hummingbird in the "brilliants", tribe Heliantheini in subfamily Lesbiinae. It is found in Ecuador and Peru.

children.name: Coeligena torquata
children.description: The collared inca is a species of hummingbird found in humid Andean forests from western Venezuela through Colombia and Ecuador to Peru. It is very distinctive in having a white chest-patch and white on the tail. Like other hummingbirds it takes energy from flower nectar, while the plant benefits from the symbiotic relationship by being pollinated.

children.name: Eurotrochilus
children.description: Eurotrochilus — a genus of hummingbirds in the family Trochilidae.

children.name: Eurotrochilus inexpectatus
children.description: Eurotrochilus is an extinct genus of stem group hummingbirds (Trochilidae) and are the closest known relatives of the crown group Trochilidae. Despite Eurotrochilus being morphologically very similar to modern hummingbirds, they still retained several primitive features and are not closely related to any specific extant hummingbird in the crown group. children.name: Mellisuga helenae
children.description: The bee hummingbird, zunzuncito or Helena hummingbird is a species of hummingbird, native to the island of Cuba in the Caribbean. It is the smallest known bird. The bee hummingbird feeds on flower nectar and insects.
